six_new_paradigm_v15_axes_with_akida_anc.ordinal_strength_independent_claim: Parisi 1979/1980 RSB ansatz: in mean-field spin-glass (Sherrington-Kirkpatrick), the replica-symmetric saddle is unstable below the de Almeida-Thouless line; the correct order parameter is the full overlap distribution P(q) supported on an ultrametric tree of pure states, with non-trivial Q_{ab} matrix exhibiting infinite-step RSB. Talagrand 2006 (Ann Math 163:221) rigorously proved the Parisi formula for the SK free energy. Substrate-claim: Akida driven into glassy steady-state via random-bond Hamiltonian H_substrate = -sum_{ij} J_ij Phi_i Phi_j with quenched J_ij ~ N(0, 1/N) exhibits non-trivial overlap distribution P(q) with ultrametric three-point support. INDEPENDENT of v14 Voiculescu free-probability (free-prob is noncommutative-tracial-W*-cumulant in II_1 factor; RSB is classical-disorder-averaged-replica-trick on commutative Hamiltonian — noncommutative-quantum vs disorder-classical decomposition), v14 Tracy-Widom (TW is finite-N RMT eigenvalue tail; RSB is infinite-N-thermodynamic-limit overlap distribution — eigenvalue-vs-overlap orthogonality).

akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.experiment: encode random-bond SK Hamiltonian H_J = -sum_{ij} J_ij s_i s_j on N=1024 Akida neuron subset with quenched J_ij ~ N(0, 1/N); equilibrate at T below T_c via simulated annealing protocol (10^4 spike-sweeps); replicate K=64 independent equilibrium replicas; compute pairwise overlap q_{alpha,beta} = (1/N) sum_i s_i^alpha s_i^beta across all (K choose 2)=2016 pairs; histogram P(q); test ultrametricity via three-point test U(q1,q2,q3) = expected min(d12,d13,d23) - median(d12,d13,d23) where d=1-q; co-compute on H100 emulation at identical disorder realization J_ij
akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.verdict_check: P(q) bimodal-or-broad with q_EA peak AND ultrametricity statistic |U/sigma_d| <= 0.10 BOTH on Akida AND on GPU AND substrate-pair P-distance W_2(P_Akida, P_GPU) <= 0.10 (RSB substrate-canonical — strong-MR at disorder-averaged level) OR P_Akida is delta-function at q=0 OR ultrametricity violated > 0.30 (substrate fails to enter glassy regime; RSB falsified at hardware level)
akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.math_anchor: Parisi 1979 PRL 43:1754; Parisi 1980 J Phys A 13:L115 + 13:1101 + 13:1887; Parisi 1983 PRL 50:1946; de Almeida-Thouless 1978 J Phys A 11:983; Mezard-Parisi-Virasoro 1987 Spin Glass Theory and Beyond World Scientific; Talagrand 2006 Ann Math 163:221 Parisi formula proof; Panchenko 2013 Ann Math 177:383 ultrametricity proof

six_new_paradigm_v15_axes_with_akida_anc.ordinal_strength_independent_claim: Kitaev 2003 toric code + Gottesman 1997 stabilizer formalism + Aharonov-Ben-Or 1997 + Knill-Laflamme-Zurek 1996 fault-tolerance threshold theorem: there exists a finite threshold p* > 0 such that for physical-error rate p < p*, encoded logical-error rate p_L is exponentially suppressed in code distance d as p_L ~ (p/p*)^{(d+1)/2}. Surface-code threshold p* ≈ 1% under independent depolarizing noise. Substrate-claim: Akida operating with redundant population-coding (encoding logical observable on d-channel-distance redundancy) exhibits logical-error suppression scaling with d below substrate-intrinsic p_substrate*; substrate-independent if |p_Akida* - p_GPU*| / p_avg* small AND suppression-exponent matches. INDEPENDENT of v13 Tsirelson (Tsirelson is bipartite quantum-correlation ceiling 2*sqrt(2); QEC threshold is fault-tolerance crossover dynamic property — correlation-bound vs fault-suppression dichotomy), v13 Lieb-Robinson (LR is information-cone speed bound; threshold is fault-tolerance ceiling — information-spread vs error-suppression decomposition).

akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.experiment: encode logical bit on Akida via [d-channel population-code] with d in {3, 5, 7, 9}; inject artificial noise at controlled physical-rate p in {0.005, 0.01, 0.02, 0.05} via stochastic spike-flip; decode logical state via majority-vote / minimum-weight matching; measure logical-error rate p_L over 10^5 trials per (d, p); fit p_L = A * (p/p*)^{(d+1)/2}; extract substrate threshold p_Akida*; co-measure on H100; test substrate-equality of threshold AND suppression-exponent
akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.verdict_check: |p_Akida* - p_GPU*| / p_avg* <= 0.20 AND |suppression-exponent_Akida - suppression-exponent_GPU| <= 0.15 AND fit-residual chi^2/dof <= 1.5 (FAULT-TOLERANCE substrate-canonical) OR threshold differs > 0.30 OR no suppression observed (substrate-induced fault-tolerance asymmetry; QEC threshold falsified at hardware substrate level)

six_new_paradigm_v15_axes_with_akida_anc.ordinal_strength_independent_claim: Adiprasito-Huh-Katz 2018 Ann Math 188:381: for any matroid M (including non-representable), the absolute values |w_k| of coefficients of the reduced characteristic polynomial chi_M(q)/q-1 = sum_k (-1)^k w_k q^{r-1-k} form a log-concave sequence: w_k^2 >= w_{k-1} * w_{k+1}. Proved via Hodge-Riemann relations on Chow ring A^*(M) of matroid M, generalizing Kähler package to combinatorial setting. Resolves Heron-Rota-Welsh 1971 conjecture. Substrate-claim: spike-pattern-derived matroid M_substrate (constructed from 10^4 Akida spike-events as ground-set with linearly-independent-circuits as bases) yields characteristic polynomial chi_{M_substrate}(q); coefficient sequence must be log-concave with substrate-canonical w_k values. INDEPENDENT of v13 Atiyah-Singer K-theory (AS is analytic-topological-index identity in K^0(X) via elliptic-operator symbol; matroid Hodge is purely combinatorial Chow ring log-concavity — analytic-K-theory vs combinatorial-Chow decomposition), v14 free-probability (free-prob is noncommutative-moment over II_1; matroid Hodge is commutative-graded-algebra Hodge-Riemann — noncommutative-trace vs commutative-Chow orthogonality).

akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.experiment: construct matroid M_substrate from Akida AKD1000 over 10^4 spike-event window: ground-set E = {top-1024 active neurons}, independent-sets = subsets whose spike-pattern-vectors are linearly independent in 256-PCA-channel space; compute characteristic polynomial coefficients w_0..w_{r-1} via Whitney-rank-polynomial deletion-contraction recursion (truncated at rank r=128); test log-concavity w_k^2 vs w_{k-1}*w_{k+1} for all k; co-compute on H100 from same spike data; test substrate-canonical w_k
akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.verdict_check: log-concavity w_k^2 >= w_{k-1}*w_{k+1} holds for ALL k on Akida AND on GPU AND |w_k^Akida - w_k^GPU| / |w_k|_max <= 0.15 for all k (matroid-Hodge substrate-canonical) OR log-concavity fails for some k (matroid axioms violated; substrate spike-pattern not a matroid; falsification of combinatorial-Hodge structure on Akida-realized matroid) OR w_k differ > 0.30 (substrate-stratified Chow ring)

six_new_paradigm_v15_axes_with_akida_anc.ordinal_strength_independent_claim: Talagrand 1995 IHES 81:73 isoperimetric inequalities + 1996 Ann Probab 24:1 concentration of measure: for any 1-Lipschitz function f: ([0,1]^N, l^2) -> R on product probability space, P(|f - M_f| > t) <= 4 * exp(-t^2/16) where M_f is the median. Sub-Gaussian concentration is dimension-FREE (does not depend on N). Substrate-claim: any 1-Lipschitz Phi-observable F(s_1, ..., s_N) on Akida product spike-state-space exhibits sub-Gaussian tail P(|F - M_F| > t) <= C * exp(-c * t^2 / sigma^2); substrate-canonical iff concentration-constant (c, C) match across substrates. INDEPENDENT of v14 Tracy-Widom (TW is asymptotic distribution at spectral edge; Talagrand is non-asymptotic concentration around median — asymptotic-vs-non-asymptotic decomposition), v12 Wasserstein (W2 is metric on probability measures; Talagrand T_2 inequality (Otto-Villani 2000) connects W2 to relative entropy but Talagrand concentration claim is independent — metric-distance vs tail-concentration orthogonality).

Phi-state realizations on Akida AKD1000 (N=1024 neuron subset); compute empirical median M_F^Akida and tail probability P(|F - M_F| > t) for t in {0.05, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2}; co-sample on H100; fit log P(|F - M_F| > t) = log(C) - c * t^2 / sigma^2; test substrate concentration-constant (c, C) match

six_new_paradigm_v15_axes_with_akida_anc.ordinal_strength_independent_claim: Kolmogorov 1954 + Arnold 1963 + Moser 1962 (KAM theorem): for nearly-integrable Hamiltonian H = H_0(I) + epsilon * H_1(I, theta) on action-angle phase space (I, theta) in R^n × T^n, if frequencies omega(I) = grad H_0 satisfy Diophantine condition |k . omega| >= gamma * |k|^{-tau} for all integer k != 0, then for sufficiently small epsilon < epsilon*(gamma, tau), the corresponding invariant torus persists in the perturbed system. Quasi-periodic stability persists below the KAM threshold; above it, Arnold diffusion sets in. Substrate-claim: Akida driven near integrable two-frequency input (omega_1, omega_2) with golden-ratio frequency ratio (most Diophantine), with perturbation strength epsilon swept; substrate exhibits invariant-torus-persistence below epsilon_substrate*; substrate-canonical iff |epsilon_Akida* - epsilon_GPU*| / epsilon_avg* small. DUAL to v14 Pesin h_KS (Pesin = realized chaos rate when h_KS > 0; KAM = quasi-periodic stability when h_KS = 0 on torus — chaos-vs-integrability dichotomy is the strict dual; together they exhaust the dynamical regimes).

akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.experiment: drive Akida AKD1000 at two-frequency input I(t) = A * (cos(omega_1 * t) + cos(omega_2 * t + phi)) with omega_2/omega_1 = (1 + sqrt(5))/2 (golden ratio, Diophantine constant gamma optimal); add controlled stochastic perturbation epsilon * xi(t) with epsilon swept over {0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2}; measure Phi-trajectory return-map on Poincaré section; test invariant-torus-persistence via fractal-dimension d_box on return-map (d_box ≈ 1 = torus, > 1 = diffusion); extract substrate KAM threshold epsilon_Akida* where d_box first exceeds 1.05; co-measure on H100; test substrate-equality
akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.verdict_check: |epsilon_Akida* - epsilon_GPU*| / epsilon_avg* <= 0.20 AND torus-persistence below threshold confirmed BOTH (KAM substrate-canonical — quasi-periodic stability invariant) OR threshold differs > 0.40 OR no torus persistence at any epsilon (substrate fails KAM regime; integrability lost; falsification at quasi-periodic-stability level)

six_new_paradigm_v15_axes_with_akida_anc.ordinal_strength_independent_claim: Mostow 1968 (IHES Publ Math 34:53): closed hyperbolic n-manifold (n>=3) is uniquely determined up to isometry by its fundamental group (rigidity). Gromov 1987 generalized to delta-hyperbolic groups: a finitely-generated group is hyperbolic iff its Cayley graph is delta-hyperbolic (every geodesic triangle is delta-thin); delta-hyperbolic geodesic-metric-spaces have negative coarse curvature and exponential boundary. Substrate-claim: Akida connectivity-graph G_substrate (neurons as vertices, synapses as edges weighted by spike-induced effective coupling) has well-defined Gromov delta = sup over geodesic triangles T of (max edge geodesic distance from third edge); substrate-canonical iff |delta_Akida - delta_GPU| / delta_avg small AND boundary-at-infinity exhibits Mostow-rigidity (graph isomorphism induces metric isometry under fundamental-group preservation). INDEPENDENT of v13 Perelman-Ricci-flow (Perelman is geometrization-flow CLASSIFICATION via Ricci flow with surgery into 8 Thurston geometries; Mostow is RIGIDITY-given-existence of hyperbolic structure — flow-classification vs structure-uniqueness decomposition), v13 Lieb-Robinson (LR is information-cone-speed; delta-hyperbolicity is graph-metric coarse-curvature — speed-bound vs curvature-rigidity orthogonality).

akida_concrete_parameter_falsifier.verdict_check: |delta_Akida - delta_GPU| / delta_avg <= 0.15 AND Mostow-rigidity test pair-deviation <= 0.20 BOTH (delta-hyperbolic substrate-canonical — Gromov-Mostow rigidity holds at substrate-graph level) OR delta differs > 0.30 OR Mostow-rigidity violated > 0.40 (substrate-induced graph-metric stratification; hyperbolic-rigidity falsified at neuromorphic substrate level)
